code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test09-OCT-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria), with additional findings of ketonuria, glucosuria, and low urine specific gravity. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). HbA1c is low, suggesting possible anemia or altered red cell turnover. Findings are consistent with renal dysfunction, possible nephrotic syndrome, and metabolic derangements; clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
